metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_2891
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig.4: "Immunofluorescence staining of cytoskeletal proteins in spines"
- Immunofluorescence staining revealed a punctate, or sometimes diffuse, distribution of myosin II in dendritic spines, dendrites, and the cell body (Figure 4, C and F). ...In spines, myosin II puncta predominantly localized to the neck and a lower part of the head, and to a lesser extent to the base.

Fig.5: "Immunogold EM of cytoskeleton-associated proteins in spines"
- "Immunogold EM revealed similar distribution of myosin II between spine compartments (Figure 5D). Thus, of 11 mushroom or thin spines analyzed, the neck (10 spines) and the lower part of the head (9 spines) were most frequently labeled, whereas the base labeling was less frequent (5 spines). However, gold particles in the neck frequently formed linear clusters, possibly reflecting formation of myosin II filaments, whereas in the head they were usually scattered as single particles (Figure 5D).". Experimental description: - Species of tissue is not stated in the paper.
- Species of over-expressed Myosin IIb; Myh10 is not stated in the publication. The authors were contacted for additional information (dd 14/3/2018, ). Answer: "They were hippocampal neurons from Rattus norvegicus."
